title: Next-level animations and unique design enrichment — no repeat patterns

requirements.summary: Add unique non-repeating micro-interaction animations to ProductCard and cart badge: magnetic hover tilt effect, liquid fill progress on 'Add to Cart' button, and numerical increment burst on cart badge
acceptanceCriteria: Product cards exhibit a subtle 3D tilt/perspective on mouse hover that is visually distinct from any existing scale or shadow transition
The 'Add to Cart' button shows a left-to-right liquid fill sweep animation on click, not a ripple or pulse
The cart badge shows a number-increment burst animation (e.g., +1 float-up) unique from the existing bounce/pulse
No animation CSS class or keyframe is shared with previously implemented animations
All new animations respect prefers-reduced-motion

file_operations.description: Add magnetic 3D tilt effect on hover using mouse position tracking and CSS transform perspective. Implement liquid fill progress animation on the 'Add to Cart' button using a ::before pseudo-element with left-to-right width animation on click. Ensure animations use unique keyframes distinct from existing scale/shadow effects and respect prefers-reduced-motion.

Add numerical increment burst animation to the cart badge. When cart count increases, display a +1 floating-up text element that fades out using a unique keyframe animation. Ensure the burst effect does not reuse existing bounce/pulse animations and respects prefers-reduced-motion.

Define new keyframes for: (1) liquid-fill sweep animation with transform translateX, (2) increment-burst animation with translateY and opacity fade. Ensure these keyframes are unique and not aliased with any existing animations. Add prefers-reduced-motion media queries for all new animations.

requirements.summary: Introduce a page-transition overlay system with a branded curtain wipe animation (200–300 ms) that sweeps from left to right on route changes, applied globally via Layout component
acceptanceCriteria: A branded green curtain wipe plays on every route change
The wipe completes within 300 ms so it does not feel sluggish
Existing per-page entrance animations are unaffected and still play after the curtain exits
The overlay is implemented in Layout.tsx or a dedicated PageTransition component, not inside individual pages
Animation is skipped when prefers-reduced-motion is set

file_operations.description: Add a page transition overlay system that displays a branded green curtain wipe animation on route changes. Use TanStack Router's useRouter hook to detect navigation events. Render a fixed full-screen overlay that sweeps in from the left edge and exits to the right within 300 ms using a unique translateX keyframe. Ensure the animation does not interfere with existing per-page entrance animations and respects prefers-reduced-motion.

Define a unique keyframe for the curtain-wipe animation using translateX from -100% to 0% (sweep in) and 0% to 100% (sweep out). Use a branded emerald-to-teal gradient background. Add prefers-reduced-motion override to skip the animation.

requirements.summary: Add a dynamic scrolling progress indicator at the very top of the viewport: a thin emerald-to-teal gradient line that fills from 0% to 100% width as the user scrolls, resets on navigation
acceptanceCriteria: A thin gradient progress bar is visible at the absolute top of the screen on all pages
Its width accurately reflects scroll percentage (0% at top, 100% at bottom of page)
The gradient uses emerald-to-teal colors distinct from the existing Header accent bar gradient
It resets to 0% on every route change
Reduced-motion users see a static full-width bar instead of animation

file_operations.description: Add a scroll progress indicator as a fixed element at the very top of the viewport (above the Header). Use a scroll event listener to calculate scroll percentage and update the width of the progress bar. Implement an emerald-to-teal gradient distinct from the Header accent bar. Reset the progress to 0% on route changes using TanStack Router's navigation events. For prefers-reduced-motion, display a static full-width bar.

Add CSS for the scroll progress indicator with a unique emerald-to-teal gradient using OKLCH colors. Ensure the gradient is visually distinct from any existing Header gradients. Add prefers-reduced-motion media query to show a static full-width bar.

requirements.summary: Enhance the AnnouncementTicker with a frosted-glass pill-shaped design, animated dot-pulse dividers, and a subtle shimmer sweep across the background every 4 seconds
acceptanceCriteria: AnnouncementTicker has a frosted-glass/pill visual style distinct from its current plain strip
Announcement items are separated by animated dot-pulse icons
A shimmer sweep plays across the ticker background on a 4-second interval
The shimmer keyframe is unique and not shared with any skeleton loader or card shimmer
prefers-reduced-motion disables the shimmer and dot-pulse while keeping the scroll

file_operations.description: Replace the plain scrolling strip with a frosted-glass pill-shaped design using backdrop-filter and rounded borders. Add animated dot-pulse dividers between announcement items using a unique pulse keyframe. Implement a shimmer sweep background animation using a linear-gradient that translates across the ticker on a 4-second interval. Ensure the shimmer keyframe is distinct from any existing skeleton or card shimmer animations. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by disabling shimmer and dot-pulse while keeping the marquee scroll.

Define unique keyframes for: (1) dot-pulse animation with scale and opacity, (2) shimmer-sweep animation using translateX with a linear-gradient. Ensure these are distinct from existing skeleton shimmer animations. Add prefers-reduced-motion overrides to disable dot-pulse and shimmer.

requirements.summary: Add a floating ambient background layer to the HomePage hero section: 6–8 large, very low-opacity morphing blob shapes in deep green and teal that slowly deform and drift
acceptanceCriteria: 6–8 morphing blob shapes are visible at very low opacity behind the hero content on HomePage
Blobs use border-radius keyframe morphing or SVG filter distortion — not the same pill/capsule float used elsewhere
Blobs do not overlap or obscure any text or CTA buttons
Animation cycles are staggered so no two blobs morph in sync
Reduced-motion: blobs render as static shapes without animation

file_operations.description: Add a floating ambient background layer to the hero section rendering 6–8 large blob shapes positioned absolutely behind all hero content. Use CSS border-radius keyframe morphing with staggered animation delays to create unique deformation patterns. Set very low opacity (3–6%) in deep green and teal colors. Ensure blobs are z-indexed below hero text and CTAs. For prefers-reduced-motion, render static shapes without morphing animation.

Define unique border-radius morphing keyframes for blob shapes using multiple border-radius percentage values to create organic deformation. Add staggered animation delays across multiple blobs. Ensure these keyframes are distinct from any existing pill/capsule float animations. Add prefers-reduced-motion override to display static shapes.

requirements.summary: Implement an interactive cursor spotlight effect on Admin Dashboard pages: a radial gradient glow that follows the mouse cursor across the page background, activated only on desktop
acceptanceCriteria: A radial emerald glow follows the mouse on all five Admin Dashboard pages on desktop
The glow is applied to the page background layer, not on top of content
Effect is disabled on touch/mobile devices (pointer: coarse)
The CSS/JS used is unique and not reused from any other animation in the codebase
prefers-reduced-motion disables the cursor tracking

file_operations.description: Add an interactive cursor spotlight effect using a mousemove event listener that updates CSS custom properties for cursor position. Render a background layer with a radial-gradient glow centered at the cursor position. Enable only on desktop (pointer: fine media query). Respect prefers-reduced-motion by disabling cursor tracking.

Add the same interactive cursor spotlight effect as AdminDashboardPage, following the mouse with a radial emerald glow on the background layer. Enable only on desktop and respect prefers-reduced-motion.

Add the same interactive cursor spotlight effect as AdminDashboardPage, following the mouse with a radial emerald glow on the background layer. Enable only on desktop and respect prefers-reduced-motion.

Add the same interactive cursor spotlight effect as AdminDashboardPage, following the mouse with a radial emerald glow on the background layer. Enable only on desktop and respect prefers-reduced-motion.

Add the same interactive cursor spotlight effect as AdminDashboardPage, following the mouse with a radial emerald glow on the background layer. Enable only on desktop and respect prefers-reduced-motion.

Add CSS for the cursor spotlight effect using radial-gradient with CSS custom properties for dynamic cursor positioning. Add media queries to disable on touch devices (pointer: coarse) and for prefers-reduced-motion.

requirements.summary: Add a staggered text word-reveal animation to all major page headings across Seller Dashboard pages: each word reveals upward from a clip-path mask with a 60 ms stagger per word
acceptanceCriteria: All h1 and major section titles on the four Seller Dashboard pages use a word-split clip-path reveal on mount
Each word reveals with a 60 ms delay after the previous word
The animation uses CSS clip-path or overflow-hidden mask — NOT the TypewriterText component
The animation triggers once on page load via IntersectionObserver
Reduced-motion: words appear instantly without reveal animation

file_operations.description: Create a custom React hook that implements word-reveal animation. Split text into individual words, wrap each in a span, and apply a clip-path reveal animation with staggered delays (60 ms per word). Use IntersectionObserver to trigger the animation once on mount. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by showing words instantly without animation.
Apply the useWordReveal hook to all h1 and major section titles on the seller dashboard. Ensure the word-reveal animation uses clip-path and is visually distinct from the TypewriterText component.

Apply the useWordReveal hook to all h1 and major section titles on the seller products page. Ensure the word-reveal animation uses clip-path and is visually distinct from the TypewriterText component.
Apply the useWordReveal hook to all h1 and major section titles on the seller orders page. Ensure the word-reveal animation uses clip-path and is visually distinct from the TypewriterText component.
Apply the useWordReveal hook to all h1 and major section titles on the seller profile page. Ensure the word-reveal animation uses clip-path and is visually distinct from the TypewriterText component.

Define a unique clip-path reveal keyframe that animates from clip-path: inset(100% 0 0 0) to clip-path: inset(0 0 0 0) for upward word reveal. Add prefers-reduced-motion override to show words instantly.

requirements.summary: Add a unique 'ink-drop' ripple animation to the StatusBadge component: concentric-ring ripple expanding outward from center when status value changes
acceptanceCriteria: StatusBadge plays a concentric ring ripple animation when its status prop changes
The ripple uses a unique keyframe (outward expanding rings fading to transparent)
Animation duration is 400–600 ms and does not shift layout
The ripple color matches the new status color
prefers-reduced-motion: badge changes color instantly without ripple

file_operations.description: Add an ink-drop ripple animation that triggers when the status prop changes. Use a pseudo-element (::after) to render concentric expanding rings that fade to transparent. Match the ripple color to the new status color. Ensure the animation uses a unique keyframe distinct from existing pulsing ring or table row sweep animations, lasts 400–600 ms, and does not shift layout. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by changing color instantly without ripple.

Define a unique keyframe for the ink-drop ripple animation using transform scale from 0 to 2.5 and opacity from 0.6 to 0. Ensure this is distinct from any existing pulse or sweep animations. Add prefers-reduced-motion override to skip the ripple.

requirements.summary: Apply a unique visual treatment to the SkeletonProductCard component: replace the current shimmer with a scan-line animation — a single bright horizontal line sweeping top-to-bottom
acceptanceCriteria: SkeletonProductCard shows a single bright scan-line sweeping top-to-bottom instead of a shimmer
The scan-line keyframe is unique (translateY-based single line, not a gradient shift)
The skeleton background uses a subtle dark-tinted green tone for the medical-scanner feel
Animation loops continuously while loading
prefers-reduced-motion: static skeleton without scan-line

file_operations.description: Replace the current shimmer animation with a scan-line animation. Use a pseudo-element (::before) to render a single bright horizontal line that sweeps top-to-bottom using translateY. Apply a dark-tinted green skeleton background for a medical-scanner aesthetic. Ensure the scan-line keyframe is unique and distinct from the existing shimmer/gradient sweep. Loop the animation continuously. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by showing a static skeleton.

Define a unique keyframe for the scan-line animation using translateY from -100% to 100% to sweep a single bright line top-to-bottom. Add a dark-tinted green background color for the medical-scanner feel. Ensure this keyframe is distinct from any shimmer or gradient shift animations. Add prefers-reduced-motion override to display static skeleton.

requirements.summary: Add a floating action button (FAB) to the CartPage with a spring-bounce entrance animation: appears bottom-right when cart has items, shows quick-checkout icon, scrolls to checkout summary on click
acceptanceCriteria: A FAB appears in the bottom-right of CartPage when cartItems.length > 0
FAB entrance uses a spring overshoot scale animation (0 → 1.2 → 1.0) distinct from the ScrollToTopButton fade-in
FAB displays a checkout/arrow-down icon clearly different from the ScrollToTopButton's up-arrow
Clicking FAB smoothly scrolls to the order summary/checkout section
FAB disappears with a scale-out when cart is empty
prefers-reduced-motion: FAB appears/disappears instantly without spring

file_operations.description: Add a floating action button (FAB) in the bottom-right corner that appears only when cartItems.length > 0. Implement a spring-bounce entrance animation using a unique keyframe with scale values (0 → 1.2 → 1.0). Display a checkout or arrow-down icon distinct from the ScrollToTopButton's up-arrow. On click, smoothly scroll to the order summary/checkout section. Implement a scale-out exit animation when the cart becomes empty. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by showing/hiding the FAB instantly without spring animation.

Define unique keyframes for: (1) spring-bounce entrance using scale values (0, 1.2, 1.0) with easing, (2) scale-out exit using scale from 1.0 to 0. Ensure these keyframes are visually distinct from the ScrollToTopButton fade-in animation. Add prefers-reduced-motion overrides to skip entrance/exit animations.
